messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0163",
  "title": "OneSight",
  "page_index": 96,
  "page_range": "96"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0164",
    "title": "一、“重角色” VS “重故事”：游戏品牌的叙事锚点分化与心智占领",
    "page_range": "96",
    "summary": "This section contrasts two divergent narrative strategies for game brand positioning and user mindshare occupation: the \"character-centric path\" (重角色路径) and the \"story-centric path\" (重故事路径). \n**Topics & Definitions:** Defines two strategic anchors for game IP cultivation. The character-centric model treats in-game characters as the primary interactive touchpoints for deep IP development, while the story-centric model binds core cultural and narrative assets to drive audience engagement.\n**Key Entities:** 《鸣潮》(Wuthering Waves) and 《原神》(Genshin Impact) exemplify the character-centric approach. 《燕云十六声》 represents the story-centric approach.\n**Procedures:** The character-centric strategy involves organizing user-generated content (UGC) campaigns synchronized with version updates and character anniversaries. The story-centric strategy utilizes overseas social media platforms to analyze and disseminate the game's historical and cultural background.\n**Claims:** The character-centric approach claims to successfully deepen long-term emotional attachment between users and the IP. The story-centric approach claims to transition users from passive players into active co-creators of the game's worldview.\n**Dates, Metrics, Tables, Figures, & Evidence Types:** None are provided. The section presents high-level strategic frameworks and operational claims without quantitative data, visual aids, specific timelines, or external empirical evidence.\nThis summary supports retrieval queries regarding comparative game branding strategies, specific title examples for each narrative model, UGC and social media operational procedures, and the intended psychological engagement outcomes of each approach."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0167",
    "title": "二、和社区玩在一起：竞技类游戏的 “玩家共创式” 品牌运营新范式",
    "page_range": "96",
    "summary": "This section outlines a \"player co-creation\" brand operation paradigm for competitive games, detailing strategies to integrate player communities into brand development and marketing. It addresses retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Topics & Procedures:** Establishing bidirectional social media communication channels to collect optimization feedback, invite core players into co-creation workflows, and report back on suggestion implementation; and linking community ecosystems with competitive tournaments by launching social media-driven gameplay creation contests, curating high-quality UGC for integration into official channels and events, and recruiting skilled players to produce and disseminate secondary content.\n- **Entities:** Social media platforms, general/core/skilled players, UGC creators, official content matrices, tournament ecosystems, and the game brand.\n- **Claims:** Maintaining transparent feedback loops and actively involving core players converts the player base into brand co-creators, while integrating community activities with tournament ecosystems and UGC strengthens player identification and community stickiness.\n- **Definitions:** Implicitly defines a \"player co-creation space\" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.\n- **Dates, Tables, Figures, Metrics, Evidence Types:** None are explicitly provided in this section."
  }
]

message.content:
This section (page 96) outlines strategic frameworks for game brand positioning and community engagement, focusing on narrative anchor differentiation and player co-creation paradigms.

**Topics & Definitions:** Contrasts the "character-centric path" (treating in-game characters as primary interactive touchpoints for IP development) with the "story-centric path" (binding core cultural and narrative assets to drive audience engagement). Implicitly defines a "player co-creation space" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.

**Entities:** 《鸣潮》(Wuthering Waves) and 《原神》(Genshin Impact) exemplify the character-centric approach; 《燕云十六声》 represents the story-centric approach. Additional entities include social media platforms, core/skilled players, UGC creators, official content matrices, competitive tournament ecosystems, and game brands.

**Procedures:** Character-centric operations involve organizing UGC campaigns synchronized with version updates and character anniversaries. Story-centric operations utilize overseas social media to analyze and disseminate historical/cultural backgrounds. Co-creation procedures establish bidirectional social media channels for feedback collection, suggestion implementation reporting, and core player workflow integration; they also link community ecosystems with tournaments via gameplay creation contests, UGC curation for official channels, and recruiting skilled players for secondary content dissemination.

**Claims:** The character-centric model claims to successfully deepen long-term emotional attachment between users and the IP. The story-centric model claims to transition users from passive players into active co-creators of the game's worldview. Transparent feedback loops and active core player involvement are claimed to convert player bases into brand co-creators, while integrating community activities with tournament ecosystems and UGC strengthens player identification and community stickiness.

**Dates, Tables, Figures, Metrics, & Evidence Types:** None are provided. The section presents high-level strategic frameworks and operational claims without quantitative data, visual aids, specific timelines, or external empirical evidence.

This summary supports retrieval queries regarding comparative game branding strategies, narrative anchor models, community co-creation paradigms, UGC and social media operational procedures, and intended psychological engagement outcomes.

response_text:
This section (page 96) outlines strategic frameworks for game brand positioning and community engagement, focusing on narrative anchor differentiation and player co-creation paradigms.

**Topics & Definitions:** Contrasts the "character-centric path" (treating in-game characters as primary interactive touchpoints for IP development) with the "story-centric path" (binding core cultural and narrative assets to drive audience engagement). Implicitly defines a "player co-creation space" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.

**Entities:** 《鸣潮》(Wuthering Waves) and 《原神》(Genshin Impact) exemplify the character-centric approach; 《燕云十六声》 represents the story-centric approach. Additional entities include social media platforms, core/skilled players, UGC creators, official content matrices, competitive tournament ecosystems, and game brands.

**Procedures:** Character-centric operations involve organizing UGC campaigns synchronized with version updates and character anniversaries. Story-centric operations utilize overseas social media to analyze and disseminate historical/cultural backgrounds. Co-creation procedures establish bidirectional social media channels for feedback collection, suggestion implementation reporting, and core player workflow integration; they also link community ecosystems with tournaments via gameplay creation contests, UGC curation for official channels, and recruiting skilled players for secondary content dissemination.

**Claims:** The character-centric model claims to successfully deepen long-term emotional attachment between users and the IP. The story-centric model claims to transition users from passive players into active co-creators of the game's worldview. Transparent feedback loops and active core player involvement are claimed to convert player bases into brand co-creators, while integrating community activities with tournament ecosystems and UGC strengthens player identification and community stickiness.

**Dates, Tables, Figures, Metrics, & Evidence Types:** None are provided. The section presents high-level strategic frameworks and operational claims without quantitative data, visual aids, specific timelines, or external empirical evidence.

This summary supports retrieval queries regarding comparative game branding strategies, narrative anchor models, community co-creation paradigms, UGC and social media operational procedures, and intended psychological engagement outcomes.
